delta: . This scattered blue light reaches observers

text: Sunlight enters the atmosphere carrying every wavelength of visible light. Air molecules scatter blue wavelengths disproportionately because Rayleigh scattering scales with the inverse fourth power of wavelength, heavily favoring shorter waves. This scattered blue light reaches observers from all directions, painting the sky blue.
